_meta.author: Claude (overnight validation batch)
_meta.note: VA Senate SD-1..5 (state tier). Evidence from party-line constitutional-amendment tallies (abortion SJ247 21-19 party-line; marriage SJ249/HJ3 with named GOP crossover lists), ReproRising F grades, NRA/VCDL ratings, bill sponsorships, direct quotes. Suetterlein flagged: voted YES on the same-sex-marriage amendment (named crossover). Cells with only party-inference or no record left null.

profile.background: VA Senate District 1 (Shenandoah Valley), since Jan 2024. Fourth-generation farmer (French Brothers Dairy / Springdale Family Farms).

timmy-french.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-1 (R), since Jan 2024. [2026-06 overnight refinement, state tier] Uniformly conservative record: voted NO on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (SJ247, party-line 21-19; graded F/'Extremist' by ReproRising) and NO on the same-sex-marriage amendment (not among the named GOP crossovers). Gun owner; in the GOP bloc opposing the 2026 assault-weapons ban (SB749). Most other dimensions rest on campaign-platform statements without an individually-confirmed roll call — left null pending cited votes.

0.note: Voted NO on the reproductive-freedom amendment (SJ247, party-line 21-19); ReproRising F/'Extremist' grade.
Voted NO on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (not among the named GOP crossovers); affirms man-woman marriage.

1.note: Opposed state codification of same-sex marriage.
Gun owner/hunter; in the GOP bloc opposing the 2026 assault-weapons ban (SB749, 21-19).

profile.background: VA Senate since 2004 (District 2; formerly 26th). Attorney; 2013 R nominee for Attorney General. Architect of Virginia's photo voter-ID law.

mark-obenshain.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-2 (R), since 2004 — deep voting record. [2026-06 overnight refinement, state tier] Reliable conservative across the board: NO on the reproductive-freedom amendment (born-alive floor quote; ChoiceTracker 'anti-choice'); NO on the same-sex-marriage amendment (not a crossover); YES on the 2016 religious-freedom bill (SB41); longtime school-choice/ESA sponsor; architect of VA photo voter-ID + SAVE-program citizenship-verification bills; tough-on-crime (bail-presumption bill, opposed qualified-immunity repeal); opposed 2026 ICE-restriction bills; opposed the 2020 red-flag law. Note: do NOT confuse with Del. Chris Obenshain (House), a relative who crossed over on marriage.

0.note: NO on the reproductive-freedom amendment; born-alive floor quote; chief-patroned a fetal-homicide bill; ChoiceTracker 'anti-choice.'
In the GOP NO bloc on the same-sex-marriage amendment; not among the 3 named crossovers.

1.note: Opposed state codification of same-sex marriage.
Voted YES on the 2016 state religious-freedom (conscience-protection) bill SB41.

0.note: Longtime sponsor of Parental Choice Education Savings Account + scholarship-tax-credit bills.

2.note: Carried 'no tax on tips'/tax-relief; abstained on the FY2026 budget as a fiscal-conservative protest against new taxes.

1.note: Architect of VA photo voter-ID law; sponsored SB1077 (SAVE-program citizenship verification) + SB1256 (photo ID).

0.note: Tough-on-crime; opposed ending qualified immunity for police; defends parole abolition.

1.note: Sponsored a bill restoring the judicial presumption against bail for violent felonies; opposes bail-leniency rollbacks.
Opposed 2026 Democratic bills restricting ICE/federal-immigration cooperation in Virginia.
Opposed the 2020 red-flag/ERPO law (due-process + 2A objection); opposes the assault-weapons ban.

profile.religion: Baptist — member and deacon, Bonsack Baptist Church (Roanoke)
profile.background: VA Senate District 3 since Jan 2024 (House of Delegates HD-17, 2012-2024). Small-business owner (in-home senior care franchise). Self-describes as 'stalwart defender of the unborn' and 'protector of the Second Amendment.'

chris-head.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-3 (R), since Jan 2024 (prior Delegate 2012-2024). [2026-06 overnight refinement, state tier] Conservative record: NO on the reproductive-freedom amendment (party-line); NO on the same-sex-marriage amendment (not a crossover) and, as a Delegate, chaired the 2022 House subcommittee that blocked a same-sex-marriage amendment; opposed the 2020 photo-voter-ID repeal (floor quote); NRA-endorsed + high VCDL rating, sponsored pro-gun bills and in the GOP bloc against the 2020 assault-weapons ban. Several dimensions returned no individually-cited vote — left null.

0.note: NO on the reproductive-freedom amendment (party-line); self-describes as 'stalwart defender of the unborn.'
NO on the same-sex-marriage amendment (not a crossover); as a Delegate chaired the 2022 subcommittee that blocked it.

1.note: Opposed state codification of same-sex marriage.
Opposed the 2020 repeal of Virginia's photo voter-ID requirement (floor quote on ballot integrity).
NRA-endorsed + high VCDL rating; sponsored pro-gun bills; in the GOP bloc opposing the assault-weapons ban.

profile.religion: Christian — New Hope Christian Church, Garden City (children's ministry)
profile.background: VA Senate District 4 (Roanoke Valley) since 2016. Realtor; Grove City College; former Roanoke County GOP chair.

david-suetterlein.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-4 (R), since 2016. [2026-06 overnight refinement, state tier] Conservative on most dimensions BUT a documented cross-party deviation on marriage: voted YES on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (HJ3, one of only 5 Republican crossovers — DeSteph, Diggs, Durant, Jordan, Suetterlein) — biblical_marriage FALSE. Otherwise: NO on the reproductive-freedom amendment (party-line); strong tax-cut record (full grocery-tax repeal, higher standard deduction, EITC); patron of a parental-notification school bill (SB817); opposed the 2026 assault-weapons ban (NRA-endorsed). Other dimensions left null pending cited votes.

0.note: NO on the reproductive-freedom amendment (party-line, both references).
Voted YES on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (HJ3) — one of only 5 Republican crossovers.

1.note: Voted to remove Virginia's same-sex-marriage ban from the constitution.
Patron of SB817 requiring schools to notify parents of course-enrollment/change procedures.

2.note: Patroned full grocery-tax repeal; preserved the higher standard deduction; made the partially-refundable EITC permanent.

1.note: Publicly opposed the 2026 assault-weapons ban; NRA-endorsed.

profile.background: VA Senate District 5 (SW Virginia/Appalachia) since a 2021 special election. Construction/real-estate entrepreneur. As Tazewell County Board chair, led a Second Amendment Sanctuary + militia ordinance.

travis-hackworth.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-5 (R), since 2021. [2026-06 overnight refinement, state tier] Strongly conservative: NO on the reproductive-freedom amendment + chief-patroned SB1284 (preborn protection / 'life at conception'); NO on the same-sex-marriage amendment (not in the 2026 crossover list) + introduced a bill to rescind the VDOE transgender model-policy mandate on schools; sponsored a school-choice scholarship-tax-credit bill (SB16); as Tazewell County chair led a 2A-Sanctuary + militia ordinance framed as resisting state/federal gun overreach. Assistant pastor (Richlands Tabernacle). Some dimensions left null pending cited votes.

0.note: NO on the reproductive-freedom amendment (named in the 19-R bloc); ReproRising F.

1.note: Chief-patroned SB1284 (2025), a preborn-protection / 'life at conception' bill.

0.note: NO on the same-sex-marriage amendment (not in the 2026 named crossover list).

1.note: Opposed state codification of same-sex marriage.

2.note: Introduced a bill to rescind the requirement that schools follow VDOE transgender model policies.

0.note: Associated with SB16 Education Improvement Scholarships Tax Credits (school choice).

3.note: As Tazewell County chair, led a 2A-Sanctuary + militia ordinance explicitly framed as resisting state/federal gun overreach (affirms state/local sovereignty).

1.note: Led the Tazewell 2A-Sanctuary effort with direct pro-gun quotes; opposes new gun restrictions.
